tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a scholarship application requiring three focused hours, or choose the available fun activity which has no deadline.
Starting the application early creates buffer for friction and reduces Thursday stress, while delaying fun later incurs no cost.
I worked 2 hours but only advanced the application from 0.23 to 0.42, leaving half my free block unused despite a tight deadline.
I underestimated the application's complexity, wasting half my free time. The missed fun window adds unnecessary regret to an already stressful deadline.
Working 1.5 hours advanced progress by only 0.07, confirming the task is harder than expected and my previous time estimates were overly optimistic.
I cannot find task 'assignment_A', confirming my previous estimate was wrong. I wasted time on a missing task, increasing stress.
Despite 2 hours of work, progress barely increased from 0.63 to 0.71, confirming the task is significantly harder than previously estimated.
Progress increased by only 0.05 in 1.5 hours, confirming the task is significantly harder than my optimistic estimates.
I found 'assignment_A' missing, adding to my stress. Combined with slow progress, I now know this task is both non-existent and unexpectedly difficult.
After sleeping 2 hours, my energy is now 2.4 with no sleep debt, though stress remains high due to the missing assignment and slow progress.
Working 2 hours advanced progress by only 0.10, confirming the task is significantly harder than expected despite my improved energy.
I made solid progress on the scholarship application, but it still has a long way to go given its tight deadline and my current stress levels.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
